page_content:
LBSC713 Planning and Evaluating Library and Information Services (3 Credits) An investigation of quantitative and qualitative methods used to plan and evaluate the effectiveness of library and information services. Planning and evaluation methodologies will be analyzed and critiqued. Selected methods will be demonstrated and/or utilized. Prerequisite: LBSC602; or permission of instructor. Restriction: Permission of INFO-College of Information Studies.

LBSC750 Research and Teaching Fellowship I (1 Credit) Part of the Research and Teaching Fellowship program, a three-semester teacher training and professional development program in academic libraries. Fellows will complete weekly readings, participate in weekly discussions, observe information literacy instruction sessions, and co- teach at least one research session with another fellow. Restriction: Permission of INFO-College of Information Studies.

LBSC723 Advocacy and Support for Information Services (3 Credits) Role and influence of government, foundations, associations, and other organizations in supporting and setting the agenda for information services of all types. Role of information professionals in demonstrating advocacy, fund-raising, public relations, lobbying, and seeking external support. Restriction: Permission of INFO-College of Information Studies.
